error_checkdeath_timeout: Target client did not respond (timeout or network error).
discord_death_embed_description: A staff member ran **Check death** on a connected player.
discord_death_killer_not_player: _Not another online player — NPC, animal, vehicle, prop, or environment._
death_detail_unknown: The game client could not classify this death from the last native read. Ask the player or check clips if it matters.
death_detail_fall: Damage from impact at height—cliffs, bad jumps, or lethal landings. Logged as environmental fall damage, not another player's weapon.
death_detail_drowning: Death in deep water or while unable to surface; may include drowning inside a submerged vehicle if the engine reports that damage type.
death_detail_bleeding: Ongoing bleeding or scripted bleed-out without a stable killer entity linked on this client.
death_detail_fire: Burning from flames or spreading fire (not necessarily a thrown explosive).
death_detail_explosion: Blast damage—dynamite, volatile compounds, or similar—as registered by the game.
death_detail_explosive: Environmental fire or blast damage; the last read did not separate fire from explosion.
death_detail_natural: World or status damage (hunger, cold, generic world) when the engine does not give a more specific label.
death_detail_vehicle_hit: Crushed or struck by a vehicle or mount as environmental damage (not necessarily a player driver tied here).
death_detail_wildlife: The registered weapon or killer points to an animal (bite or claws). No online player is listed as killer.
death_detail_stray_projectile: Projectile damage without a networked player as shooter—NPCs, distant shots, or desync can look like this.
death_detail_stray_melee: Melee damage but the aggressor is not linked here (despawned NPC, unclear sync, or stale data).
death_detail_explosive_item: Molotov, dynamite, or similar when no valid attacker ped remains on the client.
death_detail_unclear: No stable ped, vehicle, or animal weapon is tied to this death—often hunger, weather, poison, fall misreads, or engine leftovers from earlier fights.
death_detail_vehicle: Run over, rammed, or crushed by a horse, wagon, train, or other vehicle entity.
death_detail_object_trap: Props, traps, falling objects, or scripted hazards classified as an object-type killer.
death_detail_wildlife_attack: An animal ped or animal weapon hash is linked to the killing blow. Rare false positives can happen near wildlife.
death_detail_pvp: Another online player's ped is tied as killer. Name and ID come from network data at the moment of the check.
death_detail_npc_gun: A non-player character (bandit, lawman, etc.) is linked with a firearm-type weapon.
death_detail_npc_melee: Close combat from an NPC aggressor.
death_detail_npc_bow: Bow or arrow damage from an NPC.
death_detail_npc_other: NPC involvement without a clear firearm, bow, or melee group on the last read.
death_detail_weird: Entity type or weapon data from the game did not match a normal case.
death_ambient_projectile: Gunfire or arrows; no shooter linked.
